serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of grilled veal loin chop is one medium-sized chop, weighing approximately 150 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Veal, loin, grilled — Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo Food Composition Database entry for 'Veal, loin, grilled'. Energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol are exact matches. Saturated fat (3.8g/100g) is from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 17167 (Veal, loin, separable lean and fat, cooked, braised) used as the closest approved-source analogue for saturated fat breakdown not reported by HPB. Monounsaturated fat (3.8g/100g), polyunsaturated fat (0.5g/100g), and trans fat (0.0g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 17167 for veal loin, cooked, as HPB does not report these fractions.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index — Not Applicable for Negligible-Carbohydrate Foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Grilled veal loin chop contains only 0.16g carbohydrate per 100g. Per University of Sydney GI Database methodology, glycemic index testing is not performed on foods with negligible carbohydrate content. GI is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable. Glycemic load per 150g serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate contribution is negligible.

hypertension.guidance: With low sodium (86mg/100g) and a favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (229mg vs 86mg per 100g), grilled veal loin chop is suitable for individuals managing hypertension, provided no high-sodium marinades or sauces are added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and zero sugar content with high protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled veal loin chop is highly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its negligible carbohydrate content (0.16g/100g) and zero sugar, producing no glycaemic response while providing 28g/100g of high-quality protein to support satiety and glycaemic stability.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat (~3.8g/100g) and cholesterol (79mg/100g) are moderate; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of consumption in line with current dietary guidelines for saturated fat reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Arachidonic acid (omega-6) content in red meat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: As a red meat, veal contains arachidonic acid which can amplify inflammatory cytokine pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; consumption should be limited and balanced with omega-3-rich anti-inflammatory foods.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled veal loin chop is a tender, pale red meat cut from the loin portion of a young calf, typically including the bone. It is prepared by grilling until cooked through, resulting in a slightly charred exterior while remaining juicy and flavourful inside. The meat is lean muscle with some internal and external fat attached, contributing to its characteristic mild, delicate flavour and moist texture. Nutritionally, it is an excellent source of high-quality complete protein (28.04g per 100g) and is extremely low in carbohydrates (0.16g per 100g) and sugar (0g per 100g), making it well-suited for ketogenic, low-carbohydrate, and diabetic dietary patterns. Total fat is moderate at 9.48g per 100g, with saturated fat at approximately 3.8g per 100g. Sodium is low at 86mg per 100g, and the food provides meaningful potassium (229mg per 100g), calcium (17mg per 100g), iron (0.82mg per 100g), and cholesterol (79mg per 100g). Its negligible carbohydrate profile means it exerts no glycaemic impact. Clinically, it is suitable for hypertension management due to its low sodium and favourable potassium ratio, and for Type 2 Diabetes due to its absence of glycaemic load. Moderate saturated fat warrants portion awareness for cardiovascular disease patients. As a red meat, it contains arachidonic acid and should be consumed in moderation by individuals with inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient data (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) is sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo Food Composition Database, which is an approved primary source and an exact match for the food item. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (entry 17167, veal loin cooked), an approved secondary source, with the adaptation documented in notes.
notes: Saturated fat (3.8g/100g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 17167 (Veal, loin, separable lean and fat, cooked) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown separately; this is an approved secondary source substitution documented here. Monounsaturated_fat_g (3.8g/100g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.5g/100g), and trans_fat_g (0.0g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 17167 for veal loin cooked, as HPB does not report these fatty acid fractions. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient data for grilled veal loin chop is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this specific preparation, and cross-referencing USDA SR Legacy entry 17167 was used only for fatty acid fractions. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.16g/100g); glycemic_index is set to null per University of Sydney GI Database methodology for negligible-carbohydrate foods. Sugar_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source). Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source — pure muscle meat with no plant-based fibre).

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name 'Grilled veal loin chop' is precise and unambiguous. This is a Western generic food; no mandatory local-language identity applies under G6.

Nutrient source is HPB Singapore Foodo, an approved primary source, with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as approved secondary source for fatty acid fractions. Source title, organization, URL, and match type are fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (86.0), saturated_fat_g (3.8), and sugar_g (0.0) are all present as numeric values and are plausible and supported.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is null (not 0) as required for negligible-carbohydrate foods. gi_category is 'not_applicable'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. gi_source references University of Sydney GI Database methodology with full documentation of why GI testing is not applicable. All four GI fields are present.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI and label for veal chop are present. FoodEx2 code A0038 with description is present. SNOMED CT code 284140003 with description is present. All three ontology anchors are defensible and consistent.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, nutritional profile, clinical relevance for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, human_condition_name correct), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 5 entries covering canonical name, alternate English forms, and common variants. aliases_by_language english array contains all 5 entries matching the flat aliases array exactly. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Multilingual aliases not required for Western generic food.

Iteration 3: Fixed q8 failure — aliases array now contains 5 distinct entries. All entries in aliases_by_language english array verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Iteration 3: Expanded notes to document all -1 fields, confirmed-zero fields, and source substitutions per g3 and g4 requirements.
Iteration 3: Verified gi_source source_match_type is 'analogous_estimate' with full explanation of University of Sydney GI Database methodology basis.

Fatty acid fractions are from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (approved secondary source), documented in nutrient_source and notes. GI is null with gi_category not_applicable, referencing University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Aliases array contains 5 entries with full consistency between flat aliases and aliases_by_language. Ontology anchors are defensible across FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with comprehensive clinical and nutritional coverage.
